back: A finite graph is a graph that contains a finite (limited) number of vertices and a finite number of edges. The vertex set V(G) and edge set E(G) both have finite cardinalities.
An infinite graph is a graph that contains infinitely many vertices or edges. The vertex set or edge set has an infinite cardinality, making it unbounded in size.

A bipartite graph is a graph whose vertex set can be divided into two disjoint sets U and V such that every edge in the graph connects a vertex in U to a vertex in V. No edge connects vertices within the same set.

A complete bipartite graph K(m,n) is a bipartite graph where there are m vertices in one part, n vertices in the other part, and every vertex in the first set is connected to every vertex in the second set. It has m×n edges.

front: What is the condition for a graph to be bipartite?
back: A graph is bipartite if and only if it does not contain an odd cycle. This means the graph can be 2-colored (two-colorable) such that no two adjacent vertices share the same color.
A cycle graph Cn is a graph with n vertices connected in a closed chain where each vertex has degree 2. A cycle graph is bipartite only if n is even.

front: What is a matching in a bipartite graph?
back: A matching in a bipartite graph is a set of edges where no two edges share a common vertex. A maximum matching contains the maximum possible number of edges.

front: Define the complement of a bipartite graph
back: The complement of a bipartite graph G is a graph where vertices are connected if and only if they were NOT connected in G. The complement of a bipartite graph is not necessarily bipartite.

front: What is a star graph K1,n?
back: A star graph K1,n is a special complete bipartite graph where one set contains a single vertex (the center) and the other set contains n vertices. All edges connect the center to each of the n outer vertices.

front: What is the chromatic number of a bipartite graph?
back: The chromatic number of a bipartite graph is 2 (unless it has no edges, in which case it's 1). This means it requires exactly two colors to properly color the graph.
A path graph Pn is a graph with n vertices connected in a linear sequence where each vertex (except endpoints) has degree 2. A path graph is always bipartite regardless of the value of n.

front: What is a tree in terms of bipartite property?
back: A tree is an acyclic connected graph. All trees are bipartite because they contain no cycles (hence no odd cycles). Trees are a subset of bipartite graphs.

A wheel graph Wn is created by connecting a central vertex to all vertices of an n-cycle. A wheel graph is bipartite only when n is odd (the cycle part is odd, making it odd cycle = not bipartite). Wait - actually wheel graphs are never bipartite because they contain odd cycles in the rim.

front: What is the bipartite matching problem?
back: The bipartite matching problem involves finding the maximum number of pairings between two sets in a bipartite graph where each vertex is used at most once. Applications include job assignments and college admissions.
K3,3 is a complete bipartite graph with 3 vertices in each partition. It has 9 edges. It is a well-known non-planar graph (Kuratowski's theorem).
